display.purpose: Hero headlines, property titles, section headers.

shadows.name: sm
shadows.purpose: Subtle lift, list items, small raised chips.

shadows.name: lg
shadows.purpose: Modals, the toolbox panel, anything meant to feel above everything else.

shadows.name: glow
shadows.purpose: Active/focused accent elements only, never applied to neutral surfaces.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Spatial Gold Bright background, void-black text, sharp precision. Hover gets baseline Spatial Gold.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #F7C64E; color: #0e0e0e; padding: 10px 24px; border-radius: 4px; font-family: var(--font-geist-sans), system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 16px; border: none; transition: background 0.38s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), box-shadow 0.38s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #E8AE3C; box-shadow: 0 0 18px rgba(232,174,60,0.45); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 1.5px solid #E8AE3C; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: transparent; color: #E8AE3C; padding: 10px 24px; border-radius: 4px; font-family: var(--font-geist-sans), system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 16px; border: 1px solid #6E531A; transition: background 0.38s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), box-shadow 0.38s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: rgba(232, 174, 60, 0.12); box-shadow: 0 0 24px rgba(232, 174, 60, 0.18); }

components.name: Chapter Eyebrow
components.description: Numbered chapter marker that opens every section. Mono, uppercase, wide-tracked.
components.css: .ds-chapter-eyebrow { font-family: var(--font-geist-mono), 'Courier New', monospace; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.18em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #E8AE3C; border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.13); padding-bottom: 8px; }

components.name: Atmosphere Card
components.description: Ink-surface background with subtle lift, hover triggers gold glow.
components.html:
Card Title

Subtle neutral lift, hover triggers glow.
components.css: .ds-card-atmosphere { background: linear-gradient(165deg, #1a1917, #111110); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08); border-radius: 6px; padding: 24px; color: #f0ede8; transition: transform 0.38s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), box-shadow 0.38s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), border-color 0.38s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); cursor: pointer; } .ds-card-atmosphere:hover { transform: translateY(-4px); box-shadow: 0 10px 30px rgba(0,0,0,0.4), 0 0 40px rgba(232,174,60,0.05); border-color: rgba(232,174,60,0.25); } .ds-card-title { font-family: Georgia, serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: 600; margin: 0 0 8px 0; color: #f0ede8; } .ds-card-desc { font-family: var(--font-geist-sans), sans-serif; font-size: 16px; margin: 0; color: #c8c8c8; }

narrative.overview: ScoutIt isn't a real estate listings board wearing a dark theme — it's an intelligence terminal for Philippine space, built for people deciding whether to trust it enough to act. The system runs on restraint: 95% void-black canvas, 5% Spatial Gold, and every use of that gold has to earn its place. Chrome stays quiet so the actual signal — flood risk, verification badges, market data, category intelligence — can carry the visual weight instead of decoration doing it.
keyCharacteristics: Void-black canvas with tonal surface layering, not pure flat black
Gold is a signal, not wallpaper — deployed with intent, never decoratively
Mono-spaced, wide-tracked, uppercase labels for system chrome (eyebrows, badges, metadata)
Georgia serif for display headlines — editorial weight, not tech-startup sans-everywhere
Numbered chapter registry on property pages (01 — THE SPACE) is a real information architecture (ten real chapters per space category), not a decorative section marker
Scoped, purposeful glassmorphism (4px/12px/20px blur depending on context) — never default
Primary CTAs breathe with a slow gold glow pulse rather than introducing a second accent color — motion carries the 'click me' signal, gold stays the only hue

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Gold renders on ≤5% of any given screen. If a section reads gold-heavy, it's decorating, not signaling — pull it back.

rules.name: The No-Pure-Black Rule
rules.body: Never #000000 as a surface color; void-black (#0e0e0e) always carries the intended warmth. Pure black reads as an unstyled default, not a deliberate choice.

rules.name: The Instrument-Label Rule
rules.body: Any UI chrome that isn't a headline or body copy — eyebrows, badges, metadata, status labels — is mono, uppercase, and wide-tracked. This is the system's most recognizable signature; don't substitute a sans label 'for variety.'

rules.name: The Glow-Is-a-Signal Rule
rules.body: Gold glow means 'interactive and currently relevant,' not 'decorative flourish.' If glow shows up on something static, it's misused.

rules.name: The Breathing Signal Rule
rules.body: The single primary CTA on a given view breathes with a slow gold glow pulse instead of the system reaching for a second accent color to mean 'click here.'

dos: Do keep gold to ≤5% of any screen — it's a signal, not a theme color.
Do use the mono/uppercase/wide-tracked treatment for every piece of system chrome (eyebrows, badges, labels) — this is the system's core signature.
Do use tonal surface-stepping plus subtle neutral shadows for depth; reserve gold glow for genuinely interactive/focused elements only.
Do treat the numbered chapter registry as real information architecture — it's earned by actually having ten sequential chapters, not applied as decoration.
donts: Don't build generic PH listing sites (Lamudi/Zillow-style directory boards).
Don't use the 'common luxury real estate' cliché (gold-and-marble old-money tropes, ornate serif-everywhere, stock-photo-mansion aesthetic).
Don't use generic AI-template SaaS defaults (gradient hero, cream/sand backgrounds, identical card grids).
Don't use pure #000000 as a surface — void-black (#0e0e0e) always carries its intended warmth.
Don't apply gold decoratively (backgrounds, large fills, borders-for-the-sake-of-borders).
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe on cards or list items.
